# Timeline of 2010

A chronological index of notable events in the year 2010, drawn from the knowledge base.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: timeline — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->

| Date | Event | Category | Link |
|------|-------|----------|------|
| January 4, 2010 | Burj Khalifa opens — Dubai inaugurates the world's tallest building at 828 metres, surpassing Taipei 101 and redefining the limits of skyscraper engineering | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/burj-khalifa/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 12, 2010 | Haiti earthquake — magnitude 7.0 quake near Port-au-Prince kills an estimated 100,000–316,000 people | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/haiti-earthquake/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 12, 2010 | Google announces withdrawal from China — Google announces it will stop censoring search results in China, redirecting to Hong Kong servers | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/google-china-withdrawal/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 21, 2010 | Citizens United v. FEC — the US Supreme Court rules 5–4 that the First Amendment bars limits on independent political spending by corporations and unions, enabling Super PACs and reshaping US campaign finance | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/citizens-united-v-fec/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 22, 2010 | Conan O'Brien's *Tonight Show* transition — O'Brien's final *Tonight Show* airs after NBC moves to reinstate Jay Leno at 11:35 p.m.; the "#TeamConan" backlash becomes an early demonstration of social-media mobilization | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/conan-obrien-tonight-show-transition/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 25, 2010 | *Avatar* becomes highest-grossing film — James Cameron's 3D epic surpasses *Titanic* to become the highest-grossing film of all time worldwide, driving the modern 3D cinema boom | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/avatar/' | relative_url }}) |
| January 27, 2010 | J. D. Salinger dies — the reclusive author of *The Catcher in the Rye* dies at his Cornish, New Hampshire home aged 91, ending one of twentieth-century American literature's most influential and enigmatic careers | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/jd-salinger/' | relative_url }}) |
| Throughout 2010 | 3D cinema revolution — *Avatar*'s success catalyzes rapid 3D adoption across theaters and studios; 3D films earn about $1.3 billion in 2010 and establish durable premium theatrical pricing | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/3d-cinema-revolution/' | relative_url }}) |
| February 12–28, 2010 | 2010 Winter Olympics (Vancouver) — Canada hosts the Winter Games and wins a record 14 golds as host nation; Sidney Crosby's overtime goal seals men's hockey gold before 114 million viewers worldwide | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/2010-winter-olympics-vancouver/' | relative_url }}) |
| February 27, 2010 | 2010 Chile earthquake — magnitude 8.8 megathrust quake and Pacific tsunami off the Maule coast kill 525 people; one of the strongest earthquakes ever instrumentally recorded | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/2010-chile-earthquake/' | relative_url }}) |
| March 23, 2010 | Affordable Care Act signed — President Obama signs the most significant US healthcare overhaul since Medicare/Medicaid in 1965 | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/affordable-care-act/' | relative_url }}) |
| March 26, 2010 | Sinking of ROKS Cheonan — the South Korean corvette *Cheonan* breaks apart and sinks in the Yellow Sea near Baengnyeong Island, killing 46 sailors; a multinational investigation later attributes it to a North Korean torpedo | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/roks-cheonan-sinking/' | relative_url }}) |
| March 30, 2010 | Large Hadron Collider first 7 TeV collisions — CERN's LHC collides proton beams at a record 7 TeV centre-of-mass energy (3.5 TeV per beam), launching the physics run that led to the 2012 discovery of the Higgs boson | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/large-hadron-collider-7-tev-collisions/' | relative_url }}) |
| April 1, 2010 | 2010 United States Census — the decennial count's reference date records 308,745,538 residents (9.7% growth since 2000), driving congressional reapportionment toward the South and West and documenting a rapidly diversifying population | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/2010-us-census/' | relative_url }}) |
| April 3, 2010 | iPad launched — Apple releases the iPad, establishing the modern tablet computing category and selling 15 million units in its first year | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/ipad-launch/' | relative_url }}) |
| April 10, 2010 | Smolensk air disaster — a Polish Air Force Tu-154 crashes in dense fog near Smolensk, killing all 96 aboard, including President Lech Kaczyński, en route to a Katyn massacre commemoration | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/smolensk-air-disaster/' | relative_url }}) |
| April 14, 2010 | Eyjafjallajökull eruption — Iceland's Eyjafjallajökull volcano erupts, closing most European airspace and cancelling roughly 104,000 flights over six days | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/eyjafjallajokull-eruption/' | relative_url }}) |
| April 20, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon explosion — Blowout and explosion on BP's Deepwater Horizon rig kills 11 workers, beginning the largest marine oil spill in history | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/deepwater-horizon-oil-spill/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 1 – October 31, 2010 | Shanghai Expo 2010 opens — China hosts its first World's Fair under the theme "Better City, Better Life," drawing a record 73.08 million visits over 184 days, the highest attendance in Expo history | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/shanghai-expo-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 2, 2010 | European sovereign debt crisis — euro-area states and the IMF agree a €110 billion bailout for Greece, then a €750 billion eurozone backstop on May 9–10, opening the acute phase of the crisis | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/european-sovereign-debt-crisis/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 6, 2010 | 2010 United Kingdom general election — a hung parliament (Conservative 306, Labour 258, Lib Dem 57) ends 13 years of Labour rule and produces the UK's first coalition government since World War II, led by David Cameron and Nick Clegg | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/2010-uk-general-election/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 6, 2010 | Flash Crash — the Dow Jones Industrial Average falls about 998 points (9%) in roughly 36 minutes before rebounding, briefly erasing nearly $1 trillion in value in the market's first major algorithmic-trading breakdown | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/flash-crash/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 10, 2010 | 2010 Philippine presidential election — Benigno Aquino III wins on the strength of his mother Corazon Aquino's People Power legacy | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/corazon-aquino/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 17, 2010 | *Biutiful* premieres at Cannes — Alejandro González Iñárritu's Spanish-language drama debuts in competition; Javier Bardem shares the Best Actor prize and later earns the first Oscar Best Actor nomination for an all-Spanish-language role | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/biutiful/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 19, 2010 | 2010 Thai political crisis — Military crackdown ends a two-month Red Shirt occupation of Bangkok, killing at least 91 people and wounding over 1,800 | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/2010-thai-political-crisis/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 20, 2010 | First self-replicating synthetic cell — the J. Craig Venter Institute announces JCVI-syn1.0, a bacterium controlled by a chemically synthesized 1.08-million-base-pair genome | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/synthetic-cell-jcvi-syn1/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 20, 2010 | J. Craig Venter and synthetic biology leadership — geneticist J. Craig Venter leads the JCVI team publishing JCVI-syn1.0 in *Science*, framing biology as an engineering discipline and triggering an international bioethics debate | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/craig-venter-synthetic-biology/' | relative_url }}) |
| May 31, 2010 | Mavi Marmara flotilla raid — Israeli naval commandos board a Turkish aid ship bound for Gaza in international waters, killing nine activists and rupturing Israel–Turkey relations until 2016 | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/mavi-marmara-incident/' | relative_url }}) |
| June 11 – July 11, 2010 | FIFA World Cup in South Africa — First World Cup held on the African continent; Spain wins their first title | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/fifa-world-cup-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| Throughout 2010 | Global jobless recovery — world output rebounds from the 2008–09 crisis but unemployment stays persistently high (US 9.6%, eurozone 10.1%, ILO ~205 million jobless), defining a "jobless recovery" | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/global-unemployment-and-economic-recovery-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| Throughout 2010 | Twitter and social media growth — Twitter scales to about 90 million tweets per day and roughly 145 million registered users, emerging as global real-time news infrastructure and launching its Promoted Tweets advertising model | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/twitter-social-media-growth-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| June 17, 2010 | Stuxnet identified — VirusBlokAda isolates the Stuxnet worm, later revealed as the first known cyberweapon to physically sabotage industrial equipment at Iran's Natanz facility | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/stuxnet/' | relative_url }}) |
| June 18, 2010 | *Toy Story 3* released — Pixar's animated sequel opens, grossing $1.067 billion worldwide to become the highest-grossing film of 2010 and the first animated feature to surpass $1 billion | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/toy-story-3/' | relative_url }}) |
| June 24, 2010 | iPhone 4 launched — Apple releases the iPhone 4 with its 326 ppi Retina display, glass-and-steel design, front-facing camera, and FaceTime, selling 1.7 million units in the first weekend | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/iphone-4-launch/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 8, 2010 | LeBron James' "The Decision" — NBA star LeBron James announces on a live ESPN special that he will sign with the Miami Heat, inaugurating the league's modern player-empowerment superteam era | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/lebron-james-the-decision/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 15, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon well temporarily capped — After 87 days, a temporary cap stops the flow from the Macondo well | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/deepwater-horizon-oil-spill/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 16, 2010 | *Inception* released — Christopher Nolan's science-fiction thriller opens, grossing $839 million worldwide and winning 4 Academy Awards | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/inception/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 18, 2010 | Nelson Mandela's 92nd birthday and first International Mandela Day — Mandela turns 92 as the UN observes the inaugural Nelson Mandela International Day, promoting the 67-minutes community-service pledge | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/nelson-mandela-92nd-birthday/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 21, 2010 | Dodd-Frank Act signed — Most sweeping US financial regulation reforms since the Great Depression enacted in response to the 2008 financial crisis | Society & Economics | [details]({{ '/news/society-economics/dodd-frank-act/' | relative_url }}) |
| July 27, 2010 | Pakistan floods begin — Record monsoon rainfall triggers catastrophic flooding that eventually affects ~20 million people across one-fifth of Pakistan | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/pakistan-floods/' | relative_url }}) |
| July–September 2010 | Russian heat wave and wildfires — a record heat wave (44°C) and hundreds of peat and forest fires grip western Russia, causing an estimated 55,000 excess deaths and a grain-export ban that spikes global wheat prices | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/russia-heatwave-wildfires-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| August 31, 2010 | Kepler-9 multi-planet system confirmed — NASA confirms Kepler-9b and Kepler-9c, the first multiple planets observed transiting a single sun-like star, via transit timing variations | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/kepler-9-exoplanet-discovery/' | relative_url }}) |
| September 6, 2010 | *The King's Speech* premiered — Tom Hooper's historical drama premieres at the Telluride Film Festival; it would win four Academy Awards and become the most successful independent British film ever released | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/the-kings-speech/' | relative_url }}) |
| September 19, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon well permanently sealed — Macondo well permanently sealed via a relief-well "bottom kill," 152 days after the April 20 blowout | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/deepwater-horizon-macondo-well-capped/' | relative_url }}) |
| September 29, 2010 | Gliese 581g discovery announced — Astronomers announce a candidate habitable-zone exoplanet 20.3 light-years away; the widely publicized claim is later disputed and reclassified as unconfirmed | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/gliese-581g-exoplanet/' | relative_url }}) |
| October 1, 2010 | *The Social Network* released — David Fincher's film about Facebook's founding opens; wins 3 Academy Awards and grosses $225 million | Arts & Culture | [details]({{ '/news/arts-culture/the-social-network/' | relative_url }}) |
| October 5, 2010 | Graphene wins the Nobel Prize in Physics — Andre Geim and Konstantin Novoselov of the University of Manchester are awarded the physics Nobel for their 2004 isolation of graphene, launching two-dimensional materials science | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/graphene-nobel-prize-physics-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| October 6, 2010 | Instagram launched — Photo-sharing app launches on iOS, reaching 1 million users within roughly two months and pioneering the mobile-first social media era | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/instagram-launch/' | relative_url }}) |
| October 8, 2010 | Liu Xiaobo wins Nobel Peace Prize — the imprisoned Chinese dissident and Charter 08 author is awarded the prize; Beijing censors coverage and detains his wife, and his chair sits empty at the December ceremony | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/liu-xiaobo/' | relative_url }}) |
| October 13, 2010 | Chilean mining rescue — All 33 trapped miners rescued alive after 69 days underground at the San José Mine | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/chilean-mining-rescue/' | relative_url }}) |
| November 2, 2010 | 2010 US midterm elections — Republicans gain 63 House seats, the largest swing since 1948, retaking the chamber; the Tea Party movement reshapes the decade's politics | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/2010-us-midterm-elections/' | relative_url }}) |
| November 4, 2010 | Microsoft Kinect launched — Microsoft releases the controller-free Kinect motion sensor for Xbox 360, which sets a Guinness record as the fastest-selling consumer electronics device (8 million units in 60 days) | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/kinect-launch/' | relative_url }}) |
| November 13, 2010 | Aung San Suu Kyi released from house arrest — Myanmar's pro-democracy leader freed after roughly 15 years of detention, days after the junta's first multiparty elections in 20 years | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/aung-san-suu-kyi/' | relative_url }}) |
| November 28, 2010 | WikiLeaks releases diplomatic cables — WikiLeaks publishes 251,287 classified US State Department cables in major intelligence disclosure | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/wikileaks-cables/' | relative_url }}) |
| November 28, 2010 | Julian Assange and WikiLeaks — the Cablegate release brings WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange to global prominence; he surrenders to London police on December 7, opening a decade-long legal battle | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/julian-assange/' | relative_url }}) |
| December 6, 2010 | Lionel Messi named 2010 FIFA Ballon d'Or finalist — Messi heads an all-Barcelona three-man shortlist with Andrés Iniesta and Xavi; he wins the inaugural merged award at the January 10, 2011 ceremony in Zürich | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/lionel-messi-ballon-dor-2010/' | relative_url }}) |
| December 8, 2010 | SpaceX Falcon 9/Dragon COTS Demo 1 — SpaceX launches Dragon on a Falcon 9 and recovers it after two orbits, becoming the first private company to launch, orbit, and recover a spacecraft and opening the commercial-spaceflight era | Science & Technology | [details]({{ '/news/science-technology/spacex-falcon-9-dragon-cots-demo-1/' | relative_url }}) |
| December 17, 2010 | Arab Spring begins — Tunisia's Jasmine Revolution begins, setting off a wave of uprisings across the Arab world | History & Politics | [details]({{ '/news/history-politics/arab-spring-tunisia/' | relative_url }}) |
| December 17, 2010 | Mohamed Bouazizi self-immolation — Tunisian street vendor's protest in Sidi Bouzid becomes the catalyst of the Arab Spring | People | [details]({{ '/news/people/mohamed-bouazizi/' | relative_url }}) |
